reasoning: The document's fidelity composite score of 0.825 falls below the 0.85 threshold for automatic approval, particularly due to a low visual score of 0.5. While programmatic signals indicate perfect text recall, the major concerns regarding structural changes—specifically the merging of distinct objective blocks into large tables and visual content duplication—represent significant deviations from the source layout that require human verification.

concerns.analysis: The HTML output significantly alters the visual organization of the objectives and their corresponding data rows. In the source PDF, each numbered objective (e.g., 1.1, 1.2, 1.3) is presented as its own distinct block where the objective description serves as a header row directly above its specific content. In the converted HTML, multiple objectives are grouped into single large tables (e.g., 'Goal 1 Objectives and Strategies (1.1 to 1.3)'). Inside these tables, all the objective descriptions a
concerns.source_pdf_quote: PDF Pages 1-3 show objectives (e.g., 1.1, 1.2, 2.1) as individual header rows within separate table units, immediately followed by their content.
concerns.output_quote: The HTML combines objectives into groups (1.1-1.3, 1.5-1.8, 2.1-2.4) within single tables, where all objective descriptions are listed sequentially as the first several rows of the table, separated from their respective content rows below.
concerns.location: Throughout the document, specifically in the 'Goal 1 Objectives and Strategies' and 'Goal 2 Objectives and Strategies' tables.

concerns.analysis: There are several instances of duplicated body content in the HTML output. The main document title ('DRAFT' and '2021-2023... Strategic Direction') appears twice at the very beginning of the body content in different styles. Furthermore, the heading 'Goal 1. Improve, expand, and support statewide geospatial data and applications.' appears twice in the main body flow—once before the first set of objectives and again before the second set. concerns.source_pdf_quote: Document title 'DRAFT 2021-2023...' appears once as a header. 'Goal 1...' heading appears at the top of Page 1 and Page 2.
concerns.output_quote: The HTML body starts with the title twice (as headings then as plain text). The 'Goal 1...' heading is repeated twice in the body text (once before objectives 1.1-1.3 and once before 1.5-1.8).
concerns.location: Start of document and headings before tables.

action_items: Check if the consolidated table structure correctly maps objectives to strategies without losing contextual clarity
Remove redundant instances of the document title and 'Goal 1' headers
Ensure that the table headers correctly align with the numbering of the original PDF objectives
